title: Inside the Ebola Epicenter, the Virus Rages With Little to Stop It
summary: A remote gold mining town is under siege, as medical workers struggle to beat back a surge of deaths and infections.

title: Is JD Vance the 2028 Front Runner? Trump Has Questions.
summary: President Trump appears to see the matter of his heir as unsettled, adding a layer of tension to his relationship with Vice President JD Vance.

title: Judge Reopens Trump’s Lawsuit Demanding $10 Billion From IRS
summary: The ruling was a blow to both President Trump, who had voluntarily dismissed the suit last week, and to the Justice Department, which used the suit to establish a fund likely intended for Trump allies.

summary: A federal judge ordered the Kennedy Center to take President Trump’s name off the building. What happens next?

title: White House Releases Results of Trump’s Latest Physical Exam
summary: The report by President Trump’s physician said he was in “excellent health.” It showed that he had gained weight and that neurological and heart tests had come back “normal.”

title: Among Mamdani’s Priorities, Economic Development Seems Low on the List
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has not filled the vacant role of president of the Economic Development Corporation, deepening concern over his attention to the New York City economy.

title: Iran’s Hard-Liners Try to Derail Potential Deal With the U.S.
summary: A political fight is playing out in Iran, where the small but loud faction of hard-liners has used rallies, state media and private and public statements to try to undermine negotiations.

title: Middle East on Edge as Trump Mulls Decision About Iran Deal
summary: The president has wavered on whether to move ahead with an agreement with Iran to end the war. On Friday, he vowed to make a “final determination” soon.

title: Across the Middle East, Muslims Mark Eid Amid War and Crisis
summary: From Iran to Gaza, Eid al-Adha celebrations were muted as war dragged on and shortages of food and fuel roiled the region.

title: Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: ‘This Is a War’
summary: One is allied with Anthropic. The other is tied to OpenAI. They’re both spending millions to influence the midterms, and they’re leaving a trail of fearful candidates and canceled ads in their wake.

summary: Tom Steyer wants to address California’s housing crisis if he becomes governor. He is also a billionaire with extensive personal real estate holdings.

summary: Dr. Adam Hamawy, a New Jersey Democrat with big-name support on the left, has touted his humanitarian work, but some want to talk about a person he met in 1991.

title: A Clean Energy PAC Helped Beat Chip Roy, and Now It Has New Targets
summary: A PAC representing wind and solar energy interests spent $1.1 million to boost the Republican primary opponent of Chip Roy, an opponent of renewables. Now they are trying to save a Republican ally in Iowa.

title: ICE Agents to Leave Site of Volatile Protests at Detention Center
summary: Clashes between protesters and armed federal agents have erupted at the parking lot of Delaney Hall in Newark since the Memorial Day weekend.

title: Ye and Travis Scott Concerts Canceled in Italy Over Security Fears
summary: The events were canceled “to ensure public order and safety,” the authorities said. Jewish leaders had objected to the concert by Ye, formerly known as Kanye West, who has a history of antisemitic remarks.

title: As Climate Change Extends Europe’s Heat Season, Schools Bake
summary: Classrooms can be particularly vulnerable amid climate change, as the hottest times of the year increasingly overlap with the academic calendar.
